title: Creamy Indian Date Kheer
description: A creamy and sweet Indian dessert made with dates, milk, and yeast, perfect for a date night, this kheer is a fresh and chewy treat that is low in fat and rich in flavor, featuring a deglaze-fond for added depth, served in a copper mug for a touch of elegance.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine the milk, sugar, and yeast, and heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved and the yeast is activated.
Add the pitted dates to the saucepan and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are soft and the mixture is creamy.
Stir in the cardamom powder and rose water, if using, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Deglaze the saucepan with a little water to remove any fond and add it back to the kheer.
Stir in the sliced almonds and let the kheer cool to room temperature.
Cover the kheer and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Serve the kheer chilled, in a copper mug, garnished with additional sliced almonds if desired.
